steps.title: Calculate the confidence interval
steps.content:
The margin of error defines the range around the sample mean where the true population mean likely falls:
$$6.8 \pm 1.1 = [5.7, \, 7.9]$$
This interval [5.7, 7.9] contains all plausible values for the true population mean.

steps.title: Match to the correct interpretation
steps.content: Since [5.7, 7.9] is the plausible range for the true mean, any statement claiming a value outside this range is implausible. Choice A (mean < 5.7) and Choice D (mean > 7.9) both claim values outside the interval. Choice C confuses the population mean with individual employee values. Choice B correctly identifies [5.7, 7.9] as the plausible range for the true mean. That matches choice B — B is correct.
